vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: Changing the permissions of /etc/gshadow to the STIG‑specified mode is a standard file‑permission hardening step. It does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services, and does not risk locking out SSH or console access. Therefore it can be remediated safely.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential mis‑typed chmod command could set incorrect permissions
